login.title: Data Editing System Login
login.label: Login
login.summary: The entry screen where users sign in, request password-reset guidance, or start signup.
purpose: Start a system session with a login ID and password.
Use English before login, then apply the user's configured language after successful login.

layout.name: Login credentials
layout.description: Enter the login ID and password.

layout.name: Target DB area
layout.description: Shows the Target DB context available after login.

layout.name: Support actions
layout.description: Provides forgot-password, signup, and login actions.

workflow: Enter the login ID and password.
Run Login.
After login, the system loads the user language setting and Target DB context.
controls: Forgot password: shows administrator contact and request guidance.
Sign up: opens the new account request flow.
Login: signs in with the entered account.
aiGuidance: Before login, use English as the default screen language.
Do not include passwords, API keys, or other secrets in answers.

home.title: INIT Data Editing System
home.label: Main Home
home.summary: The start screen for checking the current user, role, Target DB, integrated scenario status, notices, and main shortcuts after login.

M01001.title: Project Settings
M01001.label: Project Settings
M01001.summary: Registers, reviews, updates, and deletes projects, which are the top-level work units for data editing.

M01002.title: Scenario Definition
M01002.label: Scenario Definition
M01002.summary: Defines and manages data editing scenarios under a selected project.

M02001.title: File Upload Management
M02001.label: File Upload
M02001.summary: Uploads local files into Target DB work tables and supports preview, column review, and data lookup.

M02002.title: Target Data Selection
M02002.label: Target Data
M02002.summary: Browses Target DB tables and selects the target tables used by projects and scenarios.

M03001.title: Column Type Analysis
M03001.label: Column Type Analysis
M03001.summary: Classifies columns as categorical or continuous and compares the run-time classification with the finalized column type used by downstream analysis.
purpose: Create the shared column-type contract for correlation, rule discovery, and violation detection.
Review statistics, missing values, distinct counts, and the reason for each classification.

layout.name: Run-based Column Type
layout.description: The default tab. Shows the classification produced by the selected run.

layout.name: Final Column Type
layout.description: Shows the finalized type that downstream jobs may reuse.

layout.name: Job Parameters
layout.description: Shows the actual saved parameters and result contract for the job.

workflow: Select the scenario and target table.
Choose the integrated profiling procedure or an individual model.
Save and run the job, then review the run-based type before the final type.
aiGuidance: Explain results from the selected run and its actual parameters, not only from preset JSON defaults.
Do not treat a categorical/continuous prediction as a business-approved final type without review.

M03002.title: Column Correlation Analysis
M03002.label: Column Correlation Analysis
M03002.summary: Builds one integrated relationship matrix for categorical-categorical, continuous-continuous, and categorical-continuous pairs, then derives a relationship network and clusters.
purpose: Compare all pair types with the metric appropriate to each data-type combination.
Separate passed and below-threshold pairs while retaining both in the integrated matrix.
Summarize strong relationships as network edges, nodes, and clusters.

layout.name: Integrated Relationship Matrix
layout.description: The default tab. Filters all, passed, or below-threshold pairs and updates pair-type counts together.

layout.name: Categorical Correlation
layout.description: Shows passed categorical-categorical pairs.

layout.name: Continuous Correlation
layout.description: Shows passed continuous-continuous pairs.

layout.name: Relationship Network
layout.description: Combines edge details with node and cluster summaries in one view.

deepDive: PASS_YN distinguishes pairs that passed the actual run thresholds from pairs retained for review.
Clusters are optional guidance for rule discovery and are applied only when P_CLUSTER_USAGE_MODE enables them.

M03003.title: Automatic Rule Discovery
M03003.label: Automatic Rule Discovery
M03003.summary: Runs categorical Apriori discovery and continuous LASSO plus symbolic formula discovery as one integrated job, while keeping individual models available when needed.
purpose: Discover categorical IF/THEN association rules.
Select important continuous features and generate f(x)=y symbolic rules.
Optionally prefer or restrict candidates by relationship-network cluster.

layout.name: Categorical Automatic Rules
layout.description: Combines the association model and stored rule summary into one readable rule view.

layout.name: Continuous Automatic Rules
layout.description: Compares LASSO feature importance with generated symbolic formulas and target status.

deepDive: P_RULE_PARTS selects ALL, CATEGORICAL, or CONTINUOUS work.
P_CLUSTER_USAGE_MODE supports NONE, PREFER_SAME_CLUSTER, and WITHIN_CLUSTER_ONLY. PREFER_SAME_CLUSTER is the safer default because cross-cluster predictors can remain eligible.
When cluster usage is enabled, a RELATION_NETWORK result from the same run is required.
An important LASSO feature may be absent from symbolic formulas because of R-squared, auto-target range, or feature-selection limits.
troubleshooting: If only categorical results appear, check P_RULE_PARTS, continuous target eligibility, minimum R-squared, auto-target limits, and partial-completion messages.

M03004.title: Rule Violation Detection
M03004.label: Rule Violation Detection
M03004.summary: Runs categorical association-rule checks and continuous symbolic-formula checks as one integrated job and opens the violating source rows by rule.
purpose: Detect records that contradict categorical condition/result rules.
Detect continuous values outside the configured absolute or percentage error tolerance.

layout.name: Categorical Rule Violations
layout.description: Shows candidate, exception, and violating-row counts for association rules.

layout.name: Continuous Rule Violations
layout.description: Shows predicted/actual values, error measures, and violating rows for symbolic rules.

deepDive: P_RULE_PARTS selects ALL, CATEGORICAL, or CONTINUOUS detection.
P_SYMBOLIC_RULE_TABLE_NAME refers to the symbolic rule storage table, not an association-model name or arbitrary previous result table.
P_RULE_ID=(auto) evaluates all eligible symbolic rules; a specific ID evaluates only that rule.
With P_CONTINUE_ON_ERROR=Y, one subtask may succeed while another fails, so review partial-completion details.
troubleshooting: A successful result with zero rows can mean no violation exceeded the current thresholds; distinguish it from missing rule inputs or a failed subtask.

M04001.title: Editing Scenario Design
M04001.label: Editing Scenario Design
M04001.summary: Designs, validates, and runs a reusable four-stage DAG from saved data jobs, with artifact compatibility and same-run dependencies enforced at connection time.
purpose: Connect column typing, relationship analysis, rule discovery, and violation detection as one flow.
Use four integrated jobs for the standard path while retaining individual categorical, continuous, LASSO, Apriori, symbolic, and other jobs when needed.

layout.name: Flow Designer
layout.description: Shows representative IN/OUT ports, T for table artifacts, M for model artifacts, and stage-specific connector shapes.

layout.name: Node Inspector
layout.description: Shows the selected node's actual runtime binds, upstream dependency, and result contract.

layout.name: Input and Output Contracts
layout.description: Explains artifact compatibility, REQUIRED/OPTIONAL inputs, SAME_RUN, and solid/dashed execution conditions.

deepDive: Square, triangle, circle, and diamond ports represent stages 1 through 4 respectively.
REQUIRED plus SAME_RUN means the downstream node must receive the compatible result produced earlier in the same flow run.
If P_CLUSTER_USAGE_MODE is not NONE, integrated rule discovery requires the same run's RELATION_NETWORK result.
Port shape compatibility and solid/dashed execution conditions are separate checks and both must pass validation.
troubleshooting: For a rejected connection, compare the output and input artifact names, stage shapes, REQUIRED/OPTIONAL setting, and SAME_RUN policy.

M04002.title: Editing Scenario Analysis
M04002.label: Editing Scenario Analysis
M04002.summary: Analyzes a selected flow run through stage-specific integrated result tabs for column types, relationships and clusters, automatic rules, and rule violations.

layout.name: Scenario Runs
layout.description: Selecting a run resets prior node/tab state and builds results for that run.

layout.name: Integrated Results
layout.description: Loads a tab on first use, reuses it within the same run, and preserves vertical scroll while switching tabs.

layout.name: Stage Results
layout.description: Shows integrated matrix/network, categorical/continuous automatic rules, and categorical/continuous violations.

deepDive: The integrated relationship matrix includes PASS_YN=Y and N and supports All, Passed, and Below threshold filters.
The relationship network combines edge and node/cluster information in one screen.
LASSO features are candidates; symbolic formulas are generated rules. The target status is derived from the selected run's actual parameters.
A successful violation task with zero rows is not automatically an error. Check rule counts, thresholds, and partial-completion messages.
troubleshooting: If one categorical or continuous section is missing, check P_RULE_PARTS, upstream same-run inputs, and failed-subtask details.

M05001.title: Discovered Rule Selection
M05001.label: Discovered Rule Selection
M05001.summary: A planned screen for reviewing automatically discovered rule candidates and selecting rules for actual use.

M05002.title: User Rule Registration
M05002.label: User Rule Registration
M05002.summary: A planned screen for registering manual data-quality rules or adjusting discovered rule candidates.

M06001.title: Rule Violation Data View
M06001.label: Rule Violation Data View
M06001.summary: A planned screen for reviewing detected violation records and identifying data that may need cleansing.

M06002.title: Error Data Cleansing
M06002.label: Error Data Cleansing
M06002.summary: A planned screen for cleansing, correcting, validating, and tracking changes to error or violation data.

M07001.title: Editing Effect Validation
M07001.label: Editing Effect Validation
M07001.summary: A planned screen for comparing data-quality indicators before and after cleansing to validate editing impact.

M07002.title: Final DB Apply
M07002.label: Final DB Apply
M07002.summary: A planned screen for applying validated cleansing results to the Target DB after impact and approval checks.

M07003.title: Editing History
M07003.label: Editing History
M07003.summary: A planned screen for reviewing cleansing, validation, apply, and audit history.

M90001.title: Internal Model Registration
M90001.label: Internal Model Registration
M90001.summary: Browses Target DB procedures, packages, and model objects and registers them as executable internal resources without changing object IDs or parameter names.
purpose: Standardize descriptions, result contracts, and defaults for column typing, relationship, rule, and violation procedures.
Expose accurate parameter comments in Data Work and the Flow node inspector.
Manage INIT$_SP_PREDICTED_TYPE model defaults and Target DB-specific overrides from the model resource itself.
workflow: Select a Target DB procedure or model object.
Verify that its actual parameter names and types match the preset metadata.
For INIT$_SP_PREDICTED_TYPE, review or edit Target DB overrides in Column Type Classification Settings.
Review the result table/model contract and defaults, then register the resource.
aiGuidance: P_RUN_ID and RUN_SOURCE_TYPE link results from the same Data Work or Flow run.
Deleting a column-type override restores the model default; applying preset overrides may overwrite existing Target DB overrides.
P_SYMBOLIC_RULE_TABLE_NAME identifies symbolic rule storage, not an association model.
For integrated detection, explain P_RULE_PARTS, P_CONTINUE_ON_ERROR, and P_COMMIT_YN together.

M90002.title: External Model Registration
M90002.label: External Model Registration
M90002.summary: Registers built-in Python APIs, external APIs, OML4Py resources, script-repository resources, and their call contracts and default JSON.
purpose: Manage integrated relationship, rule-discovery, and violation-detection APIs together with individual LASSO and symbolic APIs.
Keep method, endpoint, parameter names, types, defaults, descriptions, and result contracts consistent.

layout.name: API Object Groups
layout.description: Lists built-in Python API JSON groups and internal/external API resources.

layout.name: API Object Detail
layout.description: Shows resource type, method, endpoint, description, and invocation metadata.

layout.name: Parameters
layout.description: Shows immutable parameter names with their types, actual defaults, and help comments.

workflow: Select a built-in Python API or an external/OML resource.
Review method, endpoint, output mode, parameters, and default JSON.
Register it, then select the resource in M03001-M03004 Data Work and use the saved job in a Flow.
aiGuidance: Preset JSON initializes a new job; explain an executed result from that job's saved parameters and selected run.
When P_CLUSTER_USAGE_MODE is not NONE, the same run's relationship-network input is required.
P_CONTINUE_ON_ERROR=Y may produce a partial integrated result, so report successful and failed subtasks separately.

M90003.title: Model Training Management
M90003.label: Model Training Management
M90003.summary: Manages shared OML model-family training data, candidate training, validation summaries, activation, and rollback as one Target DB workflow. The current column-type family reuses confirmed labels collected across projects.
purpose: Build reusable models from user-confirmed labels collected across projects.
Train and compare candidate models without replacing the production model automatically.
Review detailed type codes together with the final categorical, continuous, and other type groups.

layout.name: Model Family Selector
layout.description: Selects the training family and Target DB, and displays the active physical model name.

layout.name: Overview
layout.description: Summarizes the active model, algorithm, version, quality metrics, and final type-group distribution.

layout.name: Training Data & Label Quality
layout.description: Reviews user-confirmed labels, duplicates, conflicts, and excluded labels.

layout.name: Train & Compare
layout.description: Configures a candidate run and compares its quality with the active model.

layout.name: Model Versions / Run History
layout.description: Reviews candidate, active, and archived versions, activation or rollback, and run history.

workflow: Select a model family and review its active model name and available training labels.
For a fresh installation with no active model or confirmed labels, run M03001 with P_PREDICTION_METHOD=ONLY_RULE, review the results, and save approved values as user-confirmed labels.
Review confirmed labels, duplicates, and conflicts in Training Data & Label Quality.
Configure algorithm, feature version, maximum input rows, and other bounded settings to train a candidate.
After candidate training, the same run automatically records accuracy, balanced accuracy, Macro F1, per-class metrics, group accuracy, and confusion metrics for comparison with the active model.
Activate only a validated candidate; roll back to a previous version when necessary.
Use Run History to review input counts, completion, and errors.
controls: Model family: selects the shared model family to train or compare.
Current Target DB: refreshes model status for the Target DB in the current login session.
Training data search: filters labels by status, final type group, and keywords.
Train candidate: trains a new candidate without changing the production model.
Compare / Activate: compares quality and activates a selected validated version.
Rollback / Archive: restores an earlier version or archives an unused one.
aiGuidance: Only labels explicitly confirmed by users are eligible for training; automatic, migrated, and unconfirmed values are excluded.

The active model name is the physical model recorded in the active-model registry. INIT$_SP_PREDICTED_TYPE reads it directly, so M03001 does not accept a model-name parameter or own a separate training job.
Column-type training uses profile features such as distinct ratio, entropy, data type, nullness, and length; it does not expose original business rows in this screen.
When quality is low or a type group lacks confirmed examples, improve label quality and sample size before training another candidate.

M91001.title: My Account
M91001.label: My Account
M91001.summary: Manages the current user's account information and personal Gemini API key status.

M91002.title: My System Settings
M91002.label: My System Settings
M91002.summary: Manages user-level UI language and display settings, the system logo, account details, email/password changes, and a personal Gemini API key.

layout.name: Setting Categories
layout.description: Lists personal setting groups and values.

layout.name: Setting Detail
layout.description: Edits key, value, description, enabled status, and order. SYSTEM_LANGUAGE provides a language selector.

layout.name: Logo Helper
layout.description: Uploads and previews SYSTEM_LOGO_IMAGE before saving the setting.

layout.name: My Account
layout.description: Manages name, email, password, and personal Gemini key status.

workflow: Select a category and edit or create a setting.
Save a language change, then log out and log in again to apply it.
For a logo, upload the image and then save the setting.
Use the account security checks when changing email or password.
aiGuidance: Passwords and Gemini keys are secrets and must not be repeated in help text, responses, or logs.
A saved language setting may take effect on the next login.

M99001.title: DB Connection Settings
M99001.label: DB Connection Settings
M99001.summary: Lets administrators register and test Target DB connections, inspect or initialize schemas, and deploy model-object groups.
aiGuidance: DB and wallet passwords are secrets managed through server-side encryption and must not be exposed in browser responses or help output.
Administrative authorization is based on the server session and role check, not on whether a menu is visible.
Schema initialization, truncation, and deployment actions require explicit impact review.

M99002.title: Database Management
M99002.label: Database Management
M99002.summary: Lets administrators browse Target DB objects and inspect table data, columns, source, and SQL results.
aiGuidance: The regular worksheet path is read-only and the server revalidates the allowed SQL scope.
Do not run data-changing SQL or DDL unless the user explicitly requests an authorized operation.

M99003.title: System Management
M99003.label: System Management
M99003.summary: Supports system initialization checks, system table review, user approval, password reset, and administrator operations.
aiGuidance: Every administrator action requires server-session role validation; hiding or showing the front-end menu is not an authorization boundary.
Treat temporary passwords as secrets and require explicit confirmation for initialization, truncation, or password-reset actions.

M99004.title: Notice Management
M99004.label: Notice Management
M99004.summary: Lets administrators create, update, delete, and attach files to notices shown on the HOME screen and related areas.
